paper.paper_title: 108 年國中教育會考英語科(閱讀)

questions.question_text: Look at the picture. The mother duck is           the baby ducks across the street.

source_meta.raw_text:
1.   Look at the picture. The mother duck is           the baby ducks across the street.
     (A) carrying
     (B) leading
     (C) pushing
     (D) watching

questions.explanation: 根據圖片情境，母鴨在帶領小鴨過馬路，「leading」意為「帶領、引導」，最符合母鴨走在前面指引小鴨的動作。故(B)正確。(A)carrying是「抱著、運送」，母鴨並未抱著小鴨；(C)pushing是「推」，不符合帶領的概念；(D)watching是「看著」，無法表達主動帶領的行為。

questions.question_text: Ms. Liu usually gives us a lot of homework,          she didn’t give us any today.

source_meta.raw_text:
2.   Ms. Liu usually gives us a lot of homework,          she didn’t give us any today.
     (A) but             (B) if               (C) or                 (D) so

questions.explanation: 本題考查連接詞的用法。句子前半「Ms. Liu usually gives us a lot of homework」與後半「she didn't give us any today」形成對比關係，前者說劉老師通常給很多功課，後者說她今天沒給，兩句意思相反，應用表示轉折的連接詞。「通常給很多功課，但今天沒給」，故 (A) but 正確。(B) if 表條件，(C) or 表選擇，(D) so 表因果，都無法表達兩句間的對比轉折關係。

questions.question_text: Playing games on the cellphone          popular with high school students.

source_meta.raw_text:
3.   Playing games on the cellphone          popular with high school students.
     (A) is             (B) are                (C) being           (D) to be

questions.explanation: 本題考查主詞與動詞的單複數一致。主詞「Playing games on the cellphone」是動名詞片語，作為單數主詞，應搭配單數動詞。句子意為「在手機上玩遊戲在高中生中很受歡迎」，空格應填入單數現在式 is，故 (A) 正確。(B) are 用於複數主詞，不符；(C) being 和 (D) to be 皆為非限定動詞形式，無法作為句子主要動詞。

questions.question_text: There        more than twenty clubs in our school. Which one would you like to join?

source_meta.raw_text:
4.   There        more than twenty clubs in our school. Which one would you like to join?
     (A) has             (B) have             (C) is              (D) are

questions.explanation: 本題考查主詞與動詞的數量一致性。主詞「more than twenty clubs」是複數名詞片語，應搭配複數動詞。「There are more than twenty clubs in our school」意為「我們學校有超過二十個社團」，故 (D) are 正確。(A) has 為第三人稱單數，(C) is 亦為單數，均與複數主詞不符；(B) have 雖為複數動詞但用於主詞為 I/you/we/they 等情況，在 There 開頭的存在句中應用 are。

questions.question_text: Diane had a bad cold last week. She is still too      to go mountain climbing with you.

source_meta.raw_text:
5.   Diane had a bad cold last week. She is still too      to go mountain climbing with you.
     (A) busy             (B) short               (C) weak           (D) young

questions.explanation: 句意為「黛安上週得了重感冒，她仍然太___而無法和你去登山」。根據上文「had a bad cold」（患重感冒），邏輯上應填入表示身體狀況不佳的詞彙，「weak」（虛弱的）最符合感冒後的生理狀態，故(C)正確。(A) busy（忙碌的）與感冒無關；(B) short（短的）語意不通；(D) young（年輕的）無法解釋為何不能登山。

questions.question_text: The book under Mary’s desk is         , but I don’t know why it is there.

source_meta.raw_text:
6.   The book under Mary’s desk is         , but I don’t know why it is there.
     (A) us            (B) me                  (C) my               (D) mine

questions.explanation: 句子結構為「The book under Mary's desk is ___」，空格後無名詞，需填入名詞性質的詞彙。根據「is」後接補語的文法，應填入名詞或名詞性代詞。mine 是所有格代詞，具名詞性質，表「我的書」，可單獨作補語；my 是所有格形容詞，後需接名詞，不能單獨使用。us 和 me 是受格代詞，不符合此句結構。故 (D) 正確。

questions.question_text:
Last night a garbage truck hit a tree and turned over. Now the street is         with
lots of garbage.

source_meta.raw_text:
7.   Last night a garbage truck hit a tree and turned over. Now the street is         with
     lots of garbage.
     (A) dark             (B) dirty             (C) heavy          (D) poor

questions.explanation: 題目描述垃圾車翻覆，垃圾散落街道，空格應填入形容詞描述街道的狀態。「the street is dirty with lots of garbage」意為「街道因大量垃圾而變得骯髒」，dirty 最符合語意，故 (B) 正確。(A) dark（黑暗的）與垃圾無關；(C) heavy（沉重的）用於形容街道不當；(D) poor（貧窮的）語意不符。

questions.question_text:
It almost killed Kevin to take care of his baby sister for one afternoon. He wondered
how his mom could do           every day.

source_meta.raw_text:
8.   It almost killed Kevin to take care of his baby sister for one afternoon. He wondered
     how his mom could do           every day.
     (A) her              (B) it                (C) one               (D) them

questions.explanation: 空格前為 do，後無受詞，需填代詞作受詞。句意為「他想知道他媽媽怎麼能每天做這件事」，「這件事」指前文「照顧妹妹一個下午」這件事，應用虛詞 it 代替，故 (B) 正確。(A) her 為人稱代詞，無法代替事物；(C) one 為數詞，不符文意；(D) them 為複數代詞，與單數事物不符。

questions.question_text: Joyce tells her brother everything. She never       anything from him.

source_meta.raw_text:
9.   Joyce tells her brother everything. She never       anything from him.
     (A) covers            (B) believes        (C) hides          (D) takes

questions.explanation: 句意為「Joyce 把所有事都告訴她哥哥，她從不向他隱瞞任何事」。根據前句「tells her brother everything」（告訴他所有事），後句應填入與「告訴」相反的動詞。hide 意為「隱瞞、隱藏」，符合邏輯對比，故 (C) 正確。(A) covers 意為「覆蓋」，(B) believes 意為「相信」，(D) takes 意為「拿取」，皆無法與前句形成合理的因果或對比關係。

questions.question_text: Ben always uses his birthday as a(n)           to ask Dad to buy him something expensive.

source_meta.raw_text:
10. Ben always uses his birthday as a(n)           to ask Dad to buy him something expensive.
    (A) action           (B) excuse               (C) prize            (D) rule

questions.explanation: 題目意思是「班總是用他的生日作為___來要求爸爸買他昂貴的東西」。根據語境，生日被當作一個藉口或理由來請求購買禮物，故(B) excuse正確。excuse意為「藉口、理由」，符合句意。(A) action（行動）語意不符；(C) prize（獎品）邏輯不通，生日不是獎品；(D) rule（規則）也不符合「用生日來要求」的含義。

questions.question_text: that last piece of pie? If not, can I have it? I didn’t eat much this morning.

source_meta.raw_text:
11.         that last piece of pie? If not, can I have it? I didn’t eat much this morning.
      (A) Had you eaten 		                        (B) Were you eating
      (C) Do you eat		                            (D) Are you going to eat

questions.explanation: 根據題幹「that last piece of pie? If not, can I have it?」的語境，說話者在詢問對方是否會吃那最後一塊派，若不吃就要求吃它，這是在詢問對方未來的意圖或計畫，故(D) Are you going to eat正確。(A)Had you eaten是過去完成式，不符合現在詢問的語境；(B)Were you eating是過去進行式，同樣指過去時間；(C)Do you eat是現在簡單式，表習慣性動作而非特定事件的未來意圖。

questions.question_text: Everyone in the class enjoys playing chess        Bill; he thinks the game is boring.

source_meta.raw_text:
12. Everyone in the class enjoys playing chess        Bill; he thinks the game is boring.
    (A) except           (B) for             (C) like              (D) of

questions.explanation: 句意為「班上每個人都喜歡下棋，除了比爾；他認為這個遊戲很無聊。」空格前後的邏輯對比顯示需要表達「除外」之意，故 (A) except 正確。(B) for 作介詞時表「為了、因為」，不符邏輯；(C) like 表「喜歡」與前文重複；(D) of 為所有格介詞，無法表達排除含義。

questions.question_text:
In my school days, I        to English radio programs every day. That was how I
learned English at that time.

source_meta.raw_text:
13. In my school days, I        to English radio programs every day. That was how I
    learned English at that time.
    (A) listen           (B) have listened    (C) used to listen (D) was listening

questions.explanation: 題幹關鍵詞為「In my school days」（在我的學生時代），表示過去某個時期的習慣性動作，需用「used to + 原動詞」表達「過去經常做某事」。句中「That was how I learned English at that time」進一步確認這是過去的習慣，故(C) used to listen正確。(A) listen為現在式，不符過去時態；(B) have listened為現在完成式，無法表達過去習慣；(D) was listening為過去進行式，表示過去某時刻正在進行，不適合表達重複習慣。

questions.question_text:
Tom’s voice      when he talks about his neighbors. He speaks faster, his face
becomes redder and you can almost see fire in his eyes.

source_meta.raw_text:
14. Tom’s voice      when he talks about his neighbors. He speaks faster, his face
    becomes redder and you can almost see fire in his eyes.
    (A) appears        (B) drops            (C) grows         (D) rises

questions.explanation: 題目描述湯姆談論鄰居時的變化：「他說話更快，臉變紅，眼睛裡幾乎能看到火焰」，這些都是情緒激動的表現。空格處應填入能表達聲音變大或升高的動詞。(D) rises 意為「上升、升高」，符合激動時聲音會提高的邏輯，故 (D) 正確。(A) appears 為「出現」，語意不符；(B) drops 為「下降」，與激動情緒相反；(C) grows 為「增長」，雖可表示變化但不如 rises 直接表達聲音升高的含義。

questions.question_text:
other waiters in the restaurant have worked here longer than Clark; only Lois
and Lana started working here before him.

source_meta.raw_text:
15.          other waiters in the restaurant have worked here longer than Clark; only Lois
      and Lana started working here before him.
      (A) All              (B) Most              (C) Some            (D) Few

questions.explanation: 題幹指出「only Lois and Lana started working here before him」，表示只有兩位員工比Clark資深，其餘員工都比他資淺。因此比Clark資深的員工數量很少，僅佔極小比例，故(D) Few正確。(A) All表示全部，與題意相反；(B) Most表示大多數，題幹明確說only兩人，不符；(C) Some雖可指少數，但通常指不確定的數量，而題幹已明確指出exactly兩人，Few更精確地表達「極少數」的概念。

media.passage_text:
Making goulash                        16. What are “Daddy and me” doing in the poem?
(A) Eating.
we don’t talk much,
(B) Singing.
Daddy and me.
(C) Dancing.
(D) Cooking.
“The talking’s in the making, son,”
he smiles.                            17. What can we most likely know about the
I see what he means.                      speaker?
(A) He seldom understands his father.
It’s in the knife                         (B) He enjoys his time with his father.
meeting the beef,                         (C) He often makes goulash for his father.
(D) He wants to be as famous as his father.
in the dancing of potatoes
 likely 可能
as they turn in the water,
in the singing
of boiling soup in the pot.
There are enough words
in the way
Daddy teaches me his famous dish
and the way he hugs me
when we finish.
Making goulash
we don’t talk much,
Daddy and me,
but everything is said.
(Ideas from Candace Pearson’s poem)
 poem 詩

OPENING HOURS
High Season (April to September): 9:00 – 18:00 every day
Low Season (October to March): 9:00 – 17:00 every day
TICKET PRICES
Line A (                    ): $20 each person

questions.question_text: Which is true about City Sight Bus tickets?
options: It costs less to buy tickets on the Internet.
People must pay the full ticket price for a pet.
Tickets are more expensive in the high season.
People save 10% if they buy two tickets for the same line.

source_meta.raw_text:
18. Which is true about City Sight Bus tickets?
    (A) It costs less to buy tickets on the Internet.
    (B) People must pay the full ticket price for a pet.
    (C) Tickets are more expensive in the high season.
    (D) People save 10% if they buy two tickets for the same line.

questions.explanation: 根據閱讀材料中「Save 10% if you buy tickets on the Internet」，網路購票可享九折優惠，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，因為材料明確指出「If you are taking your pet with you, please buy a seat for it at half price」，寵物票價為半價而非全價。(C)錯誤，票價表中Line A和Line B的價格固定，與季節無關，只有開放時間因季節而異。(D)錯誤，材料說的是「Save 10% if you buy tickets for both lines」，指購買兩條不同路線的票才享折扣，而非同一路線的兩張票。

questions.question_text: Which is true about the bus lines?
options: Both lines leave from Central Station.
There are more gardens to see on Line A.

source_meta.raw_text:
19. Which is true about the bus lines?
    (A) Line B takes more time.
    (B) Both lines cross Farmers’ Bridge.
    (C) Both lines leave from Central Station.
    (D) There are more gardens to see on Line A.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「Buses for both lines leave every hour from Central Station」，兩條路線的巴士都從中央車站每小時出發一次，故(C)正確。(A)錯誤因為Line A耗時一小時，Line B僅需50分鐘，所以Line B花費時間較少；(B)和(D)無法從材料中確認，且地圖顯示Farmers' Bridge和各花園的位置分佈不同，兩條路線不一定都經過相同景點。

media.passage_text:
Sarah: You’re VERY popular today. I’ve had seventeen calls asking for you.
Mike: Finally! After all these years of kicking and running, I’ve got my own fans!
So what did they want? My photos? Do I need to sign my name? Are they
starting a fan club for me?
Sarah: Well, let me ask you this: What day was yesterday?
Mike: Friday… Why?
Sarah: Did you forget something?
Mike: Did I forget… OH, NO! I didn’t pick up my soccer team’s sport shirts from
the shop! And we’re having this big game tomorrow!
Sarah: Yeah. And from what I’ve heard, it’s your team’s most important game this season.
Mike: OK. I have to go fix this now.
Sarah: Too late. They’ve tried. It’s not open on the weekend. And if you want to
know, they are Johnny, Ricky, Archie, Freddie…
Mike: I know, I know, everyone on my team. Oh, I’m a dead man now.
Sarah: Yeah, that was what they said on the phone, seventeen times.

questions.question_text: Why were there seventeen calls asking for Mike?
options: His team was angry at him.
He is a popular soccer player.
He did not show up for the game on Friday.
His name did not appear on the list of players.

source_meta.raw_text:
20. Why were there seventeen calls asking for Mike?
    (A) His team was angry at him.
    (B) He is a popular soccer player.
    (C) He did not show up for the game on Friday.
    (D) His name did not appear on the list of players.

questions.explanation: 根據對話，Sarah提醒Mike「Yesterday was Friday」，Mike才意識到「I didn't pick up my soccer team's sport shirts from the shop」，而且「we're having this big game tomorrow」。Sarah進一步說明「they've tried. It's not open on the weekend」，並列舉來電者都是「Johnny, Ricky, Archie, Freddie…everyone on my team」。Mike最後說「I'm a dead man now」，Sarah回應「Yeah, that was what they said on the phone, seventeen times」。這表明十七通電話都來自他的隊友，他們因為Mike忘記領取球衣而感到憤怒，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，因為他們不是粉絲而是隊友；(C)錯誤，遊戲是明天才進行；(D)無關，對話未提及名單問題。

questions.question_text: What does It mean in the dialogue?

source_meta.raw_text:
21. What does It mean in the dialogue?
    (A) The shop.		                             (B) The fan club.
    (C) The soccer game.                        (D) The soccer season.

questions.explanation: 題目問「It」在對話中指什麼。根據上文Sarah說「They've tried. It's not open on the weekend」，此句中的「It」指的是前面提到的「the shop」，因為Sarah在談論Mike沒有從店裡取回球隊球衣，而店在週末不營業，故(A)正確。(B)的fan club在對話中只是Mike的猜測，並非Sarah提及的重點；(C)和(D)都是比賽相關，但「It's not open on the weekend」明確指的是營業時間問題，只有店鋪才會有營業時間限制。

questions.question_text: What can we learn about Mike?
options: He is nice to people from his fan club.
He plays soccer with Johnny and Ricky.
He found a way to fix the trouble he made.
He decided not to go to the game on Sunday.

source_meta.raw_text:
22. What can we learn about Mike?
    (A) He is nice to people from his fan club.
    (B) He plays soccer with Johnny and Ricky.
    (C) He found a way to fix the trouble he made.
    (D) He decided not to go to the game on Sunday.

questions.explanation: 根據對話，Sarah提到「they are Johnny, Ricky, Archie, Freddie…」並說「everyone on my team」，明確指出這些人都是Mike足球隊的隊員，故(B)正確。(A)錯誤，因為這些來電者是Mike的隊友而非粉絲俱樂部成員。(C)錯誤，Mike說「I have to go fix this now」但Sarah隨即說「Too late」，表示他無法解決問題。(D)錯誤，對話未提及Mike決定不去比賽，反而暗示他必須參加這場重要比賽。

media.passage_text:
'(&

Animals have their special ways to deal with hard times in nature.
The superb fairy-wren, one kind of bright-blue bird in Australia, is one
example. This year, a ten-year study showed that this small bird has
eggs of different sizes in different kinds of weather. When the weather
is hot and dry, there is less food for young superb fairy-wrens, and they
die easily. So the mother bird will make larger eggs to help her babies
grow stronger inside before they break out of the eggs into the “hungry”
world.
However, not all mother birds are able to do this trick. Only those
with at least one male child can. When the weather is “good,”
the mother will make smaller eggs, and her sons will bring
food back for their baby brothers and sisters from the eggs.
With her sons’ help, the mother can save more energy to make
larger eggs when the weather is bad. Isn’t this amazing?
 male 雄性 energy 能量

questions.question_text: Which is the best title for this reading?
options: Australia: The Best Place for Birds.                               title 標題
Larger Mother Birds Have Larger Eggs.
Family Love: Brothers & Sisters Work Together.

source_meta.raw_text:
23. Which is the best title for this reading?
    (A) Australia: The Best Place for Birds.                               title 標題
    (B) Mother Bird Fights Weather Changes.
    (C) Larger Mother Birds Have Larger Eggs.
    (D) Family Love: Brothers & Sisters Work Together.

questions.explanation: 本文主要講述超級仙鶲母鳥根據天氣變化調整產卵大小的適應策略，文中明確指出「When the weather is hot and dry...the mother bird will make larger eggs」和「When the weather is 'good'...the mother will make smaller eggs」，核心在於母鳥如何應對天氣變化，故(B)正確。(A)過於寬泛且文中未強調澳洲特殊性；(C)曲解文意，卵的大小與母鳥體型無關，而是與天氣有關；(D)雖提及兄弟姐妹互助，但非文章重點，只是母鳥策略的輔助條件。

questions.question_text: What does this trick mean in the reading?
options: Saving food for weaker baby birds.
Helping baby birds break out of the eggs.

source_meta.raw_text:
24. What does this trick mean in the reading?
    (A) Having eggs of different sizes.
    (B) Growing stronger inside the egg.
    (C) Saving food for weaker baby birds.
    (D) Helping baby birds break out of the eggs.

questions.explanation: 根據文章，「this trick」指的是母鳥「will make larger eggs to help her babies grow stronger」的能力，也就是「在不同天氣下製造不同大小的蛋」。文中明確提到「this small bird has eggs of different sizes in different kinds of weather」，這正是該物種應對自然困難的特殊方式，故(A)正確。(B)只是蛋內發生的結果而非技巧本身；(C)文中未提及儲存食物；(D)破殼而出是自然過程，非母鳥的技巧。

questions.question_text: What can we learn about superb fairy-wrens from the reading?
options: Male birds can usually live for ten years.
Baby birds grow well in hot, dry weather.
Young male birds share the job of feeding the baby birds.
Mother birds make smaller eggs when there is less food outside.

source_meta.raw_text:
25. What can we learn about superb fairy-wrens from the reading?
    (A) Male birds can usually live for ten years.
    (B) Baby birds grow well in hot, dry weather.
    (C) Young male birds share the job of feeding the baby birds.
    (D) Mother birds make smaller eggs when there is less food outside.

questions.explanation: 根據材料，「When the weather is 'good,' the mother will make smaller eggs, and her sons will bring food back for their baby brothers and sisters from the eggs」，說明年輕雄性鳥會幫助餵養幼鳥，故(C)正確。(A)混淆了研究時間與鳥類壽命；(B)與材料相反，熱乾天氣時幼鳥容易死亡；(D)邏輯顛倒，母鳥在天氣不好時才製造較大的蛋，天氣好時製造較小的蛋。

media.passage_text:
Here are two pieces of news that Annie collected for her report.
Papier-mâché is works of art that are made of paper.
In Taiwan, it is known as zhizha and is usually burned as
a gift for dead people. Taiwan’s papier-mâché is dying
because it is not much needed now. However, this dying
art won a Frenchman’s heart when he visited the island.
He then invited one of the papier-mâché shops, Hsin
Hsin Paper Offering Store, to show their beautiful works
in France. The shop owner’s son also used papier-
mâché in a way that no one ever thought of—making a
movie with it.
Stan Lai is very important in Taiwan’s theater. He brought changes to Chinese
theater art of the old days to make it closer to our life experiences and easier to
understand. One of his most famous works is That Evening,
We Performed Crosstalk (1985). It was such a smash hit that
people became interested again in xiangsheng, a show of funny
talk between two actors. This art used to be a hobby that only
a few people shared, but Lai has opened a bigger market for it.
Now it has fans across all ages.
 work 作品

questions.question_text: What idea is talked about in both pieces of news?
options: More young people need to join in making art.
People’s ways of enjoying art change with time.
Bringing important art works to Taiwan from abroad.
Giving new life to old arts that were almost forgotten.

source_meta.raw_text:
26. What idea is talked about in both pieces of news?
    (A) More young people need to join in making art.
    (B) People’s ways of enjoying art change with time.
    (C) Bringing important art works to Taiwan from abroad.
    (D) Giving new life to old arts that were almost forgotten.

questions.explanation: 兩則新聞的共同主題是讓瀕臨失傳的傳統藝術重獲生機。第一則提到紙紮藝術「dying」但因法國人的邀請而在國際展出，第二則說相聲「used to be a hobby that only a few people shared」但因賴聲川的作品而「opened a bigger market for it」，兩者都強調將「almost forgotten」的舊藝術重新賦予活力，故(D)正確。(A)只提年輕人參與，文中未強調；(B)談藝術享受方式改變，不是核心；(C)說從國外引進作品到台灣，與材料意思相反。

questions.question_text: What does it mean when something is a smash hit?

source_meta.raw_text:
27. What does it mean when something is a smash hit?
    (A) It is old.		                       (B) It is successful.
    (C) It is foreign.		                   (D) It is useful.

questions.explanation: 根據閱讀材料，「One of his most famous works is That Evening, We Performed Crosstalk (1985). It was such a smash hit that people became interested again in xiangsheng」，由於這部作品是 Stan Lai 最著名的作品之一，且因為它的成功而使人們重新對相聲產生興趣，可知 smash hit 指的是非常成功的作品，故 (B) 正確。(A) 錯誤因為文中未提及年代久遠；(C) 錯誤因為這是台灣的作品而非外國的；(D) 錯誤因為 useful 與文意無關，重點在於作品的成功程度而非實用性。

questions.question_text: What do we know from the news?
options: Lai has taught xiangsheng in many schools.
Lai often puts his own life stories into his art works.
The owner of Hsin Hsin Paper Offering Store is a Frenchman.
People do not burn papier-mâché for dead people as often as before.

source_meta.raw_text:
28. What do we know from the news?
    (A) Lai has taught xiangsheng in many schools.
    (B) Lai often puts his own life stories into his art works.
    (C) The owner of Hsin Hsin Paper Offering Store is a Frenchman.
    (D) People do not burn papier-mâché for dead people as often as before.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「Taiwan's papier-mâché is dying because it is not much needed now」，說明紙紮藝術正在衰退是因為現在不太需要它，即人們不再像以前那樣經常燒紙紮給死人，故(D)正確。(A)材料未提及Lai在學校教授相聲；(B)材料只說他改變了傳統戲劇使其更貼近生活，未提及放入個人故事；(C)材料明確指出Frenchman邀請了Hsin Hsin店，店主是台灣人，非法國人。

media.passage_text:
VOF News                                                   16/01/2015
For many people, having a cup of coffee right out of
bed is the best way to start a day. But a report from Asap
Science says otherwise. It says the worst time for coffee is
soon after we get up. When we wake up, our bodies start
to make cortisol. Cortisol helps us to think clearly and be
quick to understand and act. The cortisol levels rise after we wake up
and climb to the highest in about an hour. So there are two problems
when we drink coffee during this time: (1) our bodies will make less
cortisol, and (2) the cortisol will make coffee less useful. And that’s why
some of us drink more and more coffee.
Then, when’s the best time to have coffee? When the cortisol levels
are going down, the report says. Cortisol levels are usually highest
between 8 a.m. and 9 a.m., between noon and 1 p.m., and between 5:30
p.m. and 6:30 p.m. So if you want to have coffee, enjoy it after these
times, and this popular drink will help you the most.
 cortisol 皮質醇 level 數值；濃度

questions.question_text: Which idea is talked about in the news?
options: How often we should drink coffee.
What happens when we have too much coffee.
How to help the body make the cortisol we need.
What happens when we have coffee at the wrong time.

source_meta.raw_text:
29. Which idea is talked about in the news?
    (A) How often we should drink coffee.
    (B) What happens when we have too much coffee.
    (C) How to help the body make the cortisol we need.
    (D) What happens when we have coffee at the wrong time.

questions.explanation: 文章核心論點為「當我們在皮質醇高峰期喝咖啡時會發生什麼」。材料明確指出：「there are two problems when we drink coffee during this time: (1) our bodies will make less cortisol, and (2) the cortisol will make coffee less useful」，說明在錯誤時間喝咖啡的負面影響，故(D)正確。(A)未提及喝咖啡的頻率；(B)未討論過量咖啡的危害；(C)與文意相反，文章是說喝咖啡會減少皮質醇分泌而非幫助製造。

questions.question_text: What does it mean when someone thinks otherwise about something?
options: They have no idea about it.
They do not think it is a problem.

source_meta.raw_text:
30. What does it mean when someone thinks otherwise about something?
    (A) They are serious about it.
    (B) They have no idea about it.
    (C) They think differently about it.
    (D) They do not think it is a problem.

questions.explanation: 題目問「think otherwise about something」的意思。根據閱讀材料第二句「But a report from Asap Science says otherwise」，此處 otherwise 表示與前述「having a cup of coffee right out of bed is the best way to start a day」相反的看法。otherwise 字面意為「另外地、不同地」，故 think otherwise 即「持不同看法、想法不同」，(C) They think differently about it 正確。(A) serious 與原意無關；(B) have no idea 表示完全不知道，過於絕對；(D) do not think it is a problem 只是不同看法的一種特例，不是 otherwise 的通用含義。

the news, which is a good time for coffee?

    the news, which is a good time for coffee?

questions.explanation: 根據文章，「When the cortisol levels are going down, the report says」，最佳喝咖啡的時間是皮質醇濃度下降的時段。文中明確指出皮質醇濃度最高的時段為上午8-9點、中午12-1點及下午5:30-6:30點，因此應在這些時段之後喝咖啡。觀察圖表，D點位於下午6:30之後，此時皮質醇濃度已開始下降，故(D)正確。A點在上午8-9點的高峰期間，B點在中午12-1點的高峰期間，C點在下午5:30-6:30的高峰期間，這三個時段都不適合喝咖啡。

questions.question_text:
Four people read the news and wrote what they thought about it.
Mike                                                        Jan 16 8:26 pm
The best time to drink coffee is when you want to drink coffee.
Sarah                                                             Jan 16 8:43 pm
I usually wake up AFTER I drink coffee!
Charlie                                                           Jan 16 8:59 pm
The worst time to drink coffee is when you're DEAD.
Lisa                                                         Jan 16 9:18 pm
If you need someone to tell you when to drink coffee, well, that’s just sad.
What did they think about the news?
options: They wanted to try what it said.
They were sad about what it said.
They did not agree with what it said.
They were not surprised at what it said.

source_meta.raw_text:
32. Four people read the news and wrote what they thought about it.




       Mike                                                        Jan 16 8:26 pm
       The best time to drink coffee is when you want to drink coffee.
       Sarah                                                             Jan 16 8:43 pm
       I usually wake up AFTER I drink coffee!
       Charlie                                                           Jan 16 8:59 pm
       The worst time to drink coffee is when you're DEAD.
       Lisa                                                         Jan 16 9:18 pm
       If you need someone to tell you when to drink coffee, well, that’s just sad.


    What did they think about the news?
    (A) They wanted to try what it said.
    (B) They were sad about what it said.
    (C) They did not agree with what it said.
    (D) They were not surprised at what it said.

questions.explanation: 四位評論者都以諷刺或反駁的語氣回應該報導。Mike說「最好的時間就是你想喝的時候」直接否定報導的建議；Sarah說「我通常在喝咖啡後才醒」嘲諷報導的邏輯；Charlie說「最糟的時間是你死了」用極端例子嘲笑；Lisa說「如果需要別人告訴你何時喝咖啡，那真可悲」質疑報導的必要性。他們都在表達不同意報導的觀點，故(C)正確。(A)錯因他們沒有表示想嘗試；(B)錯因Lisa的「sad」是指需要被告知才可悲，非對報導本身感到悲傷；(D)錯因他們的回應帶有明顯的反對態度，非無所謂。

media.passage_text:
Below is part of a new shopping center’s market report.
Our busiest time is weekend afternoons, between 1:30 p.m. and 3:30 p.m.
About half the business of the week is done then. So it would be better if
we move Weekend’s Best Buy from Saturday evening to Saturday afternoon
when there may be more shoppers during the sale. We should also think
about having more free buses during the weekend, as 75% of the shoppers
who were interviewed said two buses an hour are just not enough.
Weekend evenings are second busiest, though it is not as good as last season.
An interesting fact is that business is better on weekday evenings, usually
one hour before closing, than on weekend mornings. Most of the shoppers
then are people who live near or working parents who do their last-minute
shopping. They together make up almost 60% of the business
on weekday evenings. It might bring in more business if we
open half an hour longer during weekdays, until 10:30 p.m.
 as 由於 make up 構成；組成

questions.question_text: What does the report NOT suggest?
options: Having more free buses for shoppers.  suggest 建議
Changing the opening hours on weekdays.
Making plans to help weekday morning business.
Changing the time of the special sale on weekends.

source_meta.raw_text:
33. What does the report NOT suggest?
    (A) Having more free buses for shoppers.
                                                                    suggest 建議
    (B) Changing the opening hours on weekdays.
    (C) Making plans to help weekday morning business.
    (D) Changing the time of the special sale on weekends.

questions.explanation: 題目問報告「沒有」建議什麼。根據材料，報告明確建議三項：「move Weekend's Best Buy from Saturday evening to Saturday afternoon」（改變週末特賣時間），「having more free buses during the weekend」（增加免費巴士），以及「open half an hour longer during weekdays, until 10:30 p.m.」（延長平日營業時間）。材料提到「business is better on weekday evenings...than on weekend mornings」，但並未建議改善週日上午的生意，故(C)正確。(A)、(B)、(D)都在材料中明確提及，只有(C)沒有被建議。

questions.question_text: What does the report say about the shopping center?
options: Its business on weekdays is not as good as last season.
It has a special sale one hour before closing every day.
Most of the shoppers take the free buses to the shopping center.
A large part of its shoppers on weekday evenings are working parents.

source_meta.raw_text:

    (C) Most of the shoppers take the free buses to the shopping center.
    (D) A large part of its shoppers on weekday evenings are working parents.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「Most of the shoppers then are people who live near or working parents who do their last-minute shopping. They together make up almost 60% of the business on weekday evenings」，說明工作父母是平日傍晚購物者的主要組成，故(D)正確。(A)材料未提及平日與去年同期比較；(B)材料只提議延長營業時間，未提及每日特賣；(C)材料說75%受訪者認為巴士班次不足，並未說大多數購物者搭乘免費巴士。

media.passage_text:
Dear Maggie,
I know you won’t be happy but I still have to say this again. It hurts me
to think that you’re going to   36  . I know you’ve always wanted to be
like those actresses with big eyes and slim legs, but in fact you are already
beautiful. What you are born with makes you special because nobody
looks the same as you.
It worries me more that   37  . But sadly, there’s no quick fix to this,
not even with the doctor’s help. If you don’t try to like yourself first, no
one else will. People like you not because of your face, but because of
your heart. You need to understand this first. Until you understand this,
nothing is going to be different even if you have this change.
I truly hope you think this through again.
Love,
Linda

media.passage_text:
Elizabeth Alexandra Mary Windsor was born in London on April
21, 1926. No one thought one day she   38   the queen of the UK.
After her grandfather, King George V, died in 1936, Elizabeth’s uncle,
Prince Edward, became King Edward VIII. Edward   39   in love
with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry
her after he became the king. However, Edward was asked to choose
between the throne and his love. Finally, he decided to give up the
throne.
40  . After Edward VIII gave up the throne, Elizabeth’s father
became King George VI. As the oldest child without any brothers,
Elizabeth became next in line to the throne. She needed to say goodbye
to her good old life and prepare to be a queen. After her father died in
1952, she became Queen Elizabeth II.
Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II
41  : She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and
has visited many foreign countries. In the few hours she keeps to herself,
however, she reads and enjoys horseback riding.
 throne 王位
